title: A Quiet Place: Day One Preview Box Office Performance
text: The third installment in Paramount's post-apocalyptic franchise achieved impressive early results, earning $6.8 million at the box office in Thursday previews. This represents the franchise's best preview performance to date. The film is anticipated to open between $40 million and $50 million, which aligns with its predecessors. The original 2018 film opened to $50.2 million after earning $4.3 million in previews, while the 2021 sequel generated $48 million following $4.8 million in previews. With a production budget of $67 million, the prequel features a mostly new cast including Lupita Nyong'o, Joseph Quinn, and Alex Wolff, along with Djimon Hounsou reprising his role from the previous film. Director Michael Sarnoski, known for the Nicolas Cage thriller Pig, replaced franchise creator John Krasinski behind the camera. Set in New York City during the initial stages of an alien invasion, the story follows characters hunted by blind aliens that hunt their unlucky prey using echolocation.
